skill.name: db-connection
skill.description: Guides setup of Python and FastAPI database connections for Neon PostgreSQL, including environment configuration, SQLAlchemy engines, pooling, SSL, health checks, and Alembic integration.
skill.summary: Configure secure Neon PostgreSQL connections for Python and FastAPI applications.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed in context. No prompt injection, malicious network exfiltration, or confirmed code execution was found, but the skill provides database credential handling and command examples that require user care.

medium_findings.title: Database Credential Handling Requires Care
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE for sensitive configuration context. The skill teaches loading DB_URL from .env and SecretStr, which is legitimate for this skill but involves secrets that must not be logged or shared.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly define DB_URL handling and example environment values. This is expected behavior for a database skill, but secret exposure is a real operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Connection Test Command Can Expose Credentials
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE with limited scope. The psql example includes a full connection URL pattern, so users could paste real credentials into shell history or logs if they follow it literally.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is a legitimate database test and not automatic execution. The risk comes from user handling of live credentials in a command line.

low_findings.title: Verifier Filesystem Reads Are Local Validation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for system reconnaissance. The verifier resolves a skill path, reads SKILL.md, and checks for scripts/verify.py; no broad enumeration or data exfiltration was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script reads only the provided skill path and fixed file names. There is no network sink, subprocess call, or recursive filesystem scan.

low_findings.title: Neon Dashboard Link Is Documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for network behavior. The hardcoded Neon URL is a user-facing dashboard link, not code that sends local data to a remote service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in setup instructions and is not used by executable code. No request API or credential upload path is present near the link.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged verifier lines use regular expressions for frontmatter validation, and the SKILL.md lines contain descriptive text or connection examples, not cryptographic algorithms.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: No hashing, encryption, or weak crypto primitive appears at the flagged lines. The matches are regex and documentation content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backticks Mistaken For Ruby Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The many external command findings in SKILL.md are Markdown inline code, fenced examples, table entries, or Python snippets; they are not Ruby backtick execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is Markdown documentation, not Ruby source. The only shell command example is explicit user guidance, not hidden execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dangerous Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for malicious intent. The skill combines database credentials, a documentation link, and examples because that is its stated purpose; no exfiltration path or hidden execution was found.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context supports legitimate database setup guidance. I found no prompt injection, outbound code request, or credential collection endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: System Reconnaissance Static Findings Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines are validation output, serverless pool guidance, and SSL hostname verification, not host reconnaissance.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code and examples do not enumerate system users, processes, networks, or host metadata. They are ordinary validation and SSL settings.

content.value_statement: Database connection setup often fails because secrets, pooling, SSL, and sessions are configured in different places. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused checklist for building reliable Neon PostgreSQL connections in Python apps.
actual_capabilities: Defines Pydantic settings for DB_URL and pool configuration.
Shows SQLAlchemy engine setup with pool pre-ping and timeout settings.
Provides FastAPI session dependency patterns for database access.
Explains Neon PostgreSQL connection string and SSL requirements.
Includes health check and pool monitoring examples.
Shows Alembic integration using the same database connection logic.
limitations: It does not create database schemas or application models.
It does not perform CRUD implementation or query design.
It assumes Python, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, and Neon PostgreSQL patterns.
Users must supply and protect their own database credentials.

use_cases.title: Set up a FastAPI backend database connection
use_cases.description: Create settings, engine, and session dependency files for a new Python API.

use_cases.title: Prepare Neon PostgreSQL for production
use_cases.description: Review SSL, pooling, timeout, and connection recycling choices before deployment.

use_cases.title: Standardize database connection guidance
use_cases.description: Give a team a repeatable pattern for DB_URL handling and health checks.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Settings
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new backend project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design database settings for my FastAPI app using Neon PostgreSQL. Include DB_URL, pool settings, and safe defaults.

prompt_templates.title: Build Engine And Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline the SQLAlchemy engine and FastAPI session dependency files for my project structure.

prompt_templates.title: Review Production Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Check an existing connection setup before deployment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my database connection plan for SSL, pooling, timeouts, health checks, and secret handling.

prompt_templates.title: Integrate With Migrations
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced setup with Alembic and shared settings.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan Alembic integration that reuses my application database settings and avoids duplicate connection logic.

output_examples.input: I need a Neon PostgreSQL connection plan for a FastAPI service.
output: Recommended settings for DB_URL, pool size, overflow, timeout, and recycle values.
A file-by-file plan for settings, connection, session dependency, and health check modules.
Notes about SSL requirement, secret storage, and deployment environment variables.

output_examples.input: Check whether my current database setup is production ready.
output: A readiness review covering SSL, pool reuse, session closing, health checks, and logging risk.
A short list of changes ranked by reliability and security impact.

output_examples.input: Help me connect Alembic to the same application database settings.
output: A migration configuration approach that reuses existing settings and engine logic.
Warnings about keeping credentials out of migration logs and repository files.

best_practices: Store live database URLs only in trusted environment variable systems.
Use SSL, pool pre-ping, timeouts, and connection recycling for production databases.
Close sessions in finally blocks and expose a minimal health check.
anti_patterns: Do not commit real DB_URL values to source control.
Do not enable verbose SQL logging with sensitive production data.
Do not create a new engine for every request.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to my database automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. The user or agent must apply the changes in a project.

faq.question: Which database does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Neon PostgreSQL with Python, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, and Alembic.

faq.question: Can it help with local development settings?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes environment configuration and smaller pool settings for development.

faq.question: Does it include CRUD operations?
faq.answer: No. It is limited to connection setup, sessions, health checks, pooling, and migration integration.

faq.question: How should I handle credentials when using it?
faq.answer: Use environment variables or a secret manager. Do not paste live credentials into shared prompts, logs, or commits.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is legitimate, but it deals with database URLs and command examples that can expose secrets if mishandled.
